user.title: Connect to Adafruit IO
user.description: Enter your Adafruit IO credentials. Find your API key at **io.adafruit.com** → My Key.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach Adafruit IO — check your internet connection.

abort.already_configured: This Adafruit IO account is already configured.

init.title: AIO → HA: Select Groups
init.description: Choose which Adafruit IO feed groups to import into Home Assistant. Each group becomes a **device**; each feed becomes an **entity**.

group_feeds.title: AIO → HA: Configure feeds — {group_name}
group_feeds.description:
Configure each feed in **{group_name}**.

**Feeds:** {feeds}

**Entity types:** sensor (read-only value), switch (on/off), number (numeric input), text (string)
**Direction:** *AIO → HA* is read-only. *Bidirectional* lets HA write back to AIO.
**Unit** is optional and applies to sensor/number types.

*{remaining} group(s) remaining after this.*

ha_to_aio.title: HA → AIO: Select Entities
ha_to_aio.description:
Choose Home Assistant entities whose state changes should be pushed to Adafruit IO in real time.

The next step will let you name the AIO group and feed for each one.

ha_entity_config.title: HA → AIO: Configure — {entity_id}
ha_entity_config.description:
Set the Adafruit IO **group** and **feed** name for **{entity_id}**.

If the group or feed doesn't exist in AIO yet it will be created automatically.

*{remaining} entity(s) remaining after this.*

abort.no_groups: No Adafruit IO feed groups found. Create a group at io.adafruit.com first.
